# Rough Heston reference balls — 3 valid models (r=0, V0=theta)

This program is totally independent of Java. It exists solely to generate sets of prices
that agree across independent implementations, so the owner can use judgment in selecting
how to represent reference prices and balls that the arb4j Java implementation must satisfy.

Validity is per-model, all-or-nothing. A model is either VALID — every one of its prices
counts as one evenly-weighted sample in the reference ball — or totally INVALID and excluded
from the ball entirely. There are no per-point outliers and no outlier rejection.

VALID models (N samples per case, evenly weighted):
- Octave: sigurdroemer fractional Adams VIE (Diethelm-Ford-Freed predictor-corrector) + Lewis/Lord-Kahl Fourier inversion
- WS-Pade: fbourgey workshop Padé [n,n] rational-approx CF + Gauss-Legendre quadrature
- Julia: QuantJulia implicit fractional Adams-Moulton + Gil-Pelaez Fourier inversion

INVALID models (excluded from ball, values not recorded here):
- roughprix (hagerpa): wrapper OTM/parity bug, diverges at T=1.0
- R Adams (jgatheral): negative prices at short T (Fourier truncation)
- qipc: Riccati quadratic coefficient has extra λ² factor

Ball derivation per case:  midpoint = (1/N)Σxᵢ   spread = max|xᵢ - midpoint|   radius = spread/√N
N=2 where Octave returned no value (deep-OTM short-expiry M2 cases); N=3 elsewhere.

S0=1.0, r=0, V0=theta.
M1: H=0.12 lam=0.1 theta=0.3156 nu=0.331 rho=-0.681
M2: H=0.30 lam=2.0 theta=0.04 nu=0.3 rho=-0.7

| case_id | Octave call | WS-Pade call | Julia call | N | mid call | spread call | radius call | Octave put | WS-Pade put | Julia put | mid put | spread put | radius put |
